sections.paragraph:
θεμιτός, ή, όν (θέμις ‘law as established by custom’; Hom. Hymns, Hdt. et al.; ins, pap; Jos., Bell. 6, 432) pert. to being appropriate or right, with implication that the thing so described is not a matter of codified law but unwritten procedure based on custom and awareness of the transcendent, allowed, permitted, right θεμιτόν ἐστιν it is right w. inf. foll. (Sext. Emp., Adv. Gramm. 81; SIG 965, 16f) 1 Cl 63:1. Mostly w. neg. (Hdt. 5, 72 al.; Ael. Aristid. 45 p. 25 D.; Tob 2:13 BA; Philo, Op. M. 17 οὐ θ.; Jos., Ant. 14, 72) οὐ θ. it is not right (without copula as Hyperid. 6, 27; Just., D. 134, 3; Ath., R. 77, 29) Dg 6:10.—HVos, ΘΕΜΙΣ ’56; LfgrE col. 991 (lit.) s.v. θέμις. DDD 1613f. DELG s.v. θέμις.

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

pert. pert. = pertaining (to)

inf. inf. = infinitive

foll. foll. = followed, following

Sext. Sext. Emp. = Sextus Empiricus, II–III a.d.—List 5

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

neg. neg. = negative

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

Ael. Aristid. Ael. Aristid. = Aelius Aristides, II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

BA BA = Biblical Archaeologist—List 6

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Hyperid Hyperid , IV b.c.—List 5

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

Ath. Ath. = Athenagoras, II a.d.—List 5

Dg Dg = Letter of Diognetus, attributed to an unidentifiable apologist; II a.d.—List 1

LfgrE LfgrE = Lexicon des frühgriechischen Epos—List 6

col. col. = column

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

DDD DDD = Dictionary of Deities and Demons in the Bible—List 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 449
